name: Swings
description: Foundational kettlebell movement that develops explosive hip power and posterior chain strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, kettlebell between feet
Hinge at hips and grip the kettlebell with both hands
Hike the kettlebell back between your legs
Drive hips forward explosively to swing the bell to chest height
Let it swing back and repeat for 15–20 reps

name: Alternating Swings
description: Single-arm swing variation that adds rotational challenge and grip training.
instructions: Perform a standard two-handed swing to chest height
At the top, release with one hand and catch with the other
Let the bell swing back between your legs
Drive hips forward and repeat the hand switch each rep
Perform 10–15 reps per side

name: Side Swings
description: Lateral kettlebell swing that targets the hips and adds rotational power.
instructions: Stand with feet wider than hip-width apart
Swing the kettlebell to one side at hip height
Let it pendulum across to the other side
Rotate hips slightly in the direction of each swing
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Single Leg Deadlift
description: Unilateral hip hinge with a kettlebell that strengthens hamstrings and balance.
instructions: Stand on one leg, holding the kettlebell in the opposite hand
Hinge forward at the hips, extending the free leg behind you
Lower the kettlebell toward the floor, keeping back flat
Drive through the standing heel to return upright
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Bob and Wave
description: Dynamic full-body kettlebell movement combining a hinge with a shoulder motion.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, kettlebell in both hands
Hinge forward into a partial hip hinge (the bob)
Add a wave motion with the arms while staying in the hinge position
Keep core tight and back flat throughout
Perform for 10–15 reps

name: Lunge + Alternating Presses
description: Combines a lunge with an overhead press to train full-body coordination and strength.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell in one hand at shoulder height
Step into a lunge with the opposite leg forward
As you lower into the lunge, press the kettlebell overhead
Return to standing as you lower the bell
Alternate sides for 8–10 reps per side

name: Racked Squat
description: Squat with kettlebell in rack position that challenges core stability and leg strength.
instructions: Hold the kettlebell in rack position (resting on forearm, elbow tucked)
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Lower into a squat keeping the bell stable and torso upright
Drive through heels to return to standing
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: Goblet Squat
description: Front-loaded squat holding the kettlebell at chest height for depth and posture.
instructions: Hold kettlebell by the horns at chest height
Stand with feet slightly wider than hip-width, toes out
Lower into a deep squat keeping elbows inside your knees
Keep chest up and heels flat on the floor
Drive through heels to stand — 10–15 reps

name: Dual Front Squat
description: Double kettlebell front squat for increased load and upper back engagement.
instructions: Clean two kettlebells into rack position at shoulder height
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Lower into a deep squat keeping bells stable in rack
Drive through heels to return to standing
Perform 6–10 reps

name: Lunge
description: Kettlebell lunge that adds load for greater leg and glute development.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell in each hand hanging at sides or one in rack position
Step one foot forward and lower back knee toward the floor
Keep front knee above ankle and torso upright
Push through front heel to return to start
Perform 8–12 reps per side

name: Alternating Lunges
description: Alternating forward lunges with kettlebell for leg endurance and balance.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell in each hand hanging at sides
Step forward with one leg into a lunge
Lower until back knee nearly touches the floor
Push back to standing and immediately lunge with the other leg
Continue alternating for 10–12 reps per side

name: Side Lunges
description: Lateral lunge with kettlebell that targets inner thighs and glutes.
instructions: Hold kettlebell at chest height or hanging in front
Step wide to one side, bending the stepping knee
Lower hips toward the floor while keeping the other leg straight
Push off the bent leg to return to center
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Reverse Lunge Swings
description: Combines a reverse lunge with a kettlebell swing for power and coordination.
instructions: Start standing with kettlebell in both hands
Step back into a reverse lunge as you hike the bell between your legs
Drive the front foot into the floor and extend the hip to come out of the lunge
Use the hip drive to swing the bell to chest height
Alternate legs for 8–10 reps per side

name: Lunges Around the World
description: Full lunge circuit covering forward, lateral, and reverse directions.
instructions: Hold kettlebells at sides
Lunge forward with one leg and return to center
Lunge to the side with the same leg and return to center
Lunge backward with the same leg and return to center
Complete all directions on one side, then switch — 3 rounds per side

name: Wood Chopper Lunges
description: Lunge combined with a rotational wood chop for full-body movement.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell with both hands above one shoulder
Step into a lunge as you bring the bell diagonally to the opposite hip
Keep core engaged and rotate through the torso
Return to standing as you raise the bell back overhead
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Clean Arm Press
description: Two-phase kettlebell movement combining a clean and a press for power and strength.
instructions: Hinge at hips with kettlebell between legs
Drive hips forward and pull the bell into rack position (the clean)
From rack, press the bell overhead until arm is fully extended
Lower back to rack, then to starting position
Perform 6–8 reps per side

name: Horizontal Presses
description: Horizontal pushing exercise with kettlebells to develop chest and triceps.
instructions: Lie on a bench or floor holding a kettlebell in each hand at chest level
Start with elbows at about 45° from the body
Press both bells straight up until arms are extended
Lower with control back to chest level
Perform 8–12 reps

name: Shoulder Presses
description: Overhead press with kettlebell for shoulder and upper body strength.
instructions: Hold kettlebell in rack position at shoulder height
Brace core and keep elbow tucked below the bell
Press the bell straight overhead until arm is fully extended
Lower slowly back to rack position
Perform 8–10 reps per side

name: Pullovers
description: Lying pullover with kettlebell that stretches the chest and strengthens the lats.
instructions: Lie on a bench with head near the edge, holding kettlebell above chest
Keeping arms slightly bent, lower the bell behind your head
Feel the stretch across chest and lats
Pull the bell back over chest to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps

name: Rows
description: Single-arm kettlebell row that builds upper back and bicep strength.
instructions: Place one hand and knee on a bench for support
Hold kettlebell in the other hand, arm extended toward floor
Pull the bell toward your hip, driving elbow back
Keep back flat and avoid rotating the torso
Lower with control — 8–12 reps per side

name: Upright Rows
description: Vertical pull targeting the upper traps and side deltoids.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells or barbell in front of thighs
Pull weight straight up along your body toward the chin
Lead with your elbows, keeping them above the wrists
Do not pull higher than the chin
Lower with control — 10–12 reps

name: Side Front Raises
description: Combination of lateral and front raises to develop shoulder endurance.
instructions: Stand holding a kettlebell in each hand at sides
Raise one arm to the side and the other to the front simultaneously
Lift to shoulder height with a slight elbow bend
Lower with control and alternate arms
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Rotation
description: Kettlebell rotation exercise that develops core rotational strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding kettlebell at chest
Rotate the torso to one side, bringing the bell along
Keep hips square and feet flat on the floor
Return to center and rotate to the other side
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Windmills
description: Complex movement that develops shoulder stability, hip mobility, and core strength.
instructions: Press kettlebell overhead with one arm, feet shoulder-width apart
Turn feet 45° away from the arm holding the bell
Hinge sideways at the hip, lowering the free hand toward the floor
Keep eyes on the kettlebell throughout
Return to standing — 5–8 reps per side

name: Uppercuts
description: Boxing-inspired kettlebell movement that trains rotational power and shoulder strength.
instructions: Stand in a slight squat stance, holding a kettlebell in one hand
Drive the bell upward in an uppercut arc, rotating through the hips
Let hips and core generate the power, not just the arm
Control the bell back to starting position
Perform 10–12 reps per side

name: Helos
description: Circular overhead kettlebell movement that challenges shoulder stability and core control.
instructions: Press the kettlebell overhead with one arm
Move the bell in a controlled circle overhead (halo motion)
Keep the shoulder packed and core braced
Complete 5 circles in one direction, then reverse
Perform per side with fully controlled movement

name: Kniend Pushout Rotate
description: Kneeling kettlebell push-and-rotate exercise for core anti-rotation strength.
instructions: Kneel on one knee, holding kettlebell at chest with both hands
Push the bell straight out in front with arms extended
Rotate the bell to the left then to the right while arms stay extended
Return to chest and repeat
Perform 8–10 reps per side
